data.title: Syngenta Adopts SAP AI Solutions To Boost Farming Efficiency
data.description:
SAP SE ADS (NYSE:SAP) on Thursday disclosed a multi-year strategic technology partnership with agricultural innovation company Syngenta to advance AI-driven innovation across Syngenta's worldwide operations.

Details

The partnership will integrate artificial intelligence throughout Syngenta's operations, modernizing processes and accelerating innovation through advanced data analytics.

The financial terms of the contract were not disclosed.

Syngenta will implement SAP Cloud ERP Private solutions to modernize operations across its value chain, enhancing flexibility, resilience, and scalability, while aiming to accelerate innovation, reinforce its agricultural leadership, and protect operations against market volatility.

With SAP Business Data Cloud, the company will establish a secure, unified, and scalable data foundation to enable real-time decision-making and AI integration.

Leveraging SAP Business AI and tools like the Joule copilot, Syngenta will make faster, more informed decisions, boost operational efficiency, and provide growers with improved products and services while maintaining control and privacy over their data.

With this deal, Syngenta aims to leverage AI tools enterprise-wide to support feeding an estimated 10 billion people by 2050.

SAP will report fourth-quarter earnings results on January 29.

SAP Technical Analysis

SAP SE (SAP) is currently positioned below its key moving averages, indicating a bearish trend. The stock is trading 2.1% below its 20-day SMA, 2.7% below its 50-day SMA, and significantly below its 100-day and 200-day SMAs, which are 7.2% and 12.6% below, respectively.

The RSI is at 42.68, suggesting a neutral momentum phase. This level indicates that SAP SE’s stock is neither overbought nor oversold, which could mean it's consolidating before making a more decisive move.

Currently, MACD is above its signal line, indicating some bullish momentum. This could suggest a potential for upward movement if the stock can maintain this momentum and break through resistance levels.

Traders should watch the support level at $234.50 and the resistance level at $246.50 closely. A breach below support could signal further downside, while a move above resistance might indicate a trend reversal.

In September, a death cross occurred when the 50-day SMA crossed below the 200-day SMA, which is a bearish signal. This crossover has contributed to the current downward trend and should be monitored for its implications on future price action.

Over the past 12 months, SAP SE’s stock has declined by 8.75%, reflecting a longer-term bearish trend. This performance highlights the challenges the stock has faced, especially after reaching a 52-week high of $313.28 in July and then dropping to a low of $233.51 in November.

Currently, SAP SE’s stock is trading near the lower end of its 52-week range, at about 6.1% above its recent low. This positioning suggests that the stock is under pressure and may need to show strength to regain higher levels.

SAP Price Action: Sap shares were down 0.06% at $238.74 during premarket trading on Thursday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Photo via Shutterstock

data.title: How Web3 Incentives Could Transform Online Casino Engagement
data.description:
The online casino industry is evolving rapidly, and operators are exploring new ways to engage and retain players beyond traditional promotions. Historically, casinos have relied on bonuses, loyalty points, and advertising to attract users. While effective in the short term, these methods often encourage passive engagement, with limited long-term impact on retention or revenue. As the digital entertainment landscape becomes more competitive, operators are turning to innovative approaches that integrate technology, behavioral science, and gamified experiences. Among these, Web3-enabled incentive frameworks are emerging as a promising strategy to reshape player engagement and unlock new avenues for monetization.

One illustrative example comes from High Roller Technologies, Inc. (NYSE:ROLR), a publicly listed operator of premium online casino brands. The company is experimenting with next-generation engagement models through a partnership with Power Protocol. This collaboration aims to explore how mission-based rewards, behavioral incentives, and co-created user experiences can be responsibly deployed across regulated markets. By moving away from passive promotion, High Roller hopes to create more meaningful interactions with players, potentially enhancing retention and generating incremental revenue streams.

The Evolution of Online Casino Engagement

Online casinos have long relied on a combination of promotional offers and loyalty programs to drive activity. Free spins, deposit bonuses, and tiered reward structures remain staples of the industry. While these incentives can be effective for short-term engagement, they often fail to foster long-term loyalty. Users may engage only to capitalize on a bonus before moving on, leaving operators with limited insight into actual behavior or preference.

The rise of mobile gaming, live dealer experiences, and increasingly sophisticated online platforms has also shifted consumer expectations. Players today seek more than just monetary rewards; they want interactive, personalized, and immersive experiences that provide a sense of achievement and connection. This shift has prompted operators to consider behavioral and mission-based incentives, which reward players for completing specific actions, achieving milestones, or participating in co-created experiences. Unlike traditional promotions, these strategies aim to strengthen the connection between the operator and the player, improving retention and lifetime value.

Web3 Incentives as a Game-Changer

Web3 technologies, which include blockchain-based rewards and tokenized experiences, offer tools to implement these new engagement models at scale. Incentives can be tailored to individual behaviors, enabling operators to offer personalized rewards and gamified experiences that reflect player preferences. Co-created experiences, for example, allow users to participate in shaping certain aspects of gameplay or rewards, fostering a sense of ownership and deeper engagement.

For operators, the appeal of Web3 extends beyond engagement metrics. By integrating tokenized rewards and behavioral incentives, casinos can create additional revenue streams, encourage repeat play, and gather insights into player behavior that were previously difficult to quantify. These models also allow operators to differentiate themselves in increasingly crowded markets, providing a technological and experiential edge over competitors relying solely on traditional promotions.

Regulatory compliance is an important consideration in deploying these technologies. Operators must ensure that incentive mechanisms adhere to responsible gaming standards and regional licensing requirements. This is particularly critical in regulated markets, where misaligned incentives could risk regulatory scrutiny or harm the player experience. Companies must strike a balance between innovation and compliance, ensuring that Web3 tools enhance engagement without introducing undue risk or complexity for players.

The collaboration between High Roller and Power Protocol serves as a practical example of this trend. By leveraging the incentive infrastructure provided by Power Protocol, High Roller is testing mission-based rewards and co-created experiences across its portfolio of brands, including High Roller and Fruta. The initiative is designed to be consumer-friendly and compliant, demonstrating how operators can innovate responsibly while exploring the potential of Web3 tools. It also reflects a broader industry pattern, with leading operators increasingly integrating emerging technologies to enhance user experiences and drive growth in regulated markets.

Implications for Investors and the Industry

For investors, these developments highlight a broader shift in the iGaming sector toward innovative engagement strategies that could influence long-term revenue and market positioning. Companies that adopt next-generation incentive models early may gain a competitive advantage, offering unique experiences that differentiate them from rivals.

Increased player engagement through behavioral incentives can also translate into higher lifetime value and more predictable revenue streams. Operators can more accurately assess retention trends, forecast revenue potential, and reduce reliance on costly player acquisition campaigns. For investors, these factors contribute to longer-term visibility into growth prospects, which can be particularly valuable in a sector subject to regulatory pressures and intense competition.

The initiative also underscores the importance of partnerships in driving innovation. By leveraging specialized expertise in incentive design, operators can accelerate experimentation without overhauling their core platforms. Investors observing these efforts may interpret them as signals of proactive management strategy and forward-looking growth planning, rather than short-term marketing efforts.

The broader implications for the industry are significant. As more operators experiment with Web3-enabled engagement models, the market could see a shift toward data-driven, personalized, and interactive player experiences, with long-term benefits for revenue and retention. Companies that fail to adapt may struggle to compete in a landscape where user experience and behavioral engagement are increasingly central to success.

Challenges and Adoption Considerations

Despite the potential benefits, there are challenges to widespread adoption. Regulatory frameworks vary by jurisdiction, and operators must ensure that any Web3-enabled incentive system complies with local laws and responsible gaming standards. Players may also be unfamiliar with Web3 concepts or hesitant to engage with tokenized rewards. Achieving adoption may require education and seamless integration with existing platforms.

Operationally, scaling these engagement models requires careful planning and data-driven design. Rewards must be meaningful, achievable, and compliant, while avoiding unintended consequences such as encouraging compulsive play. Companies that balance innovation with compliance and user experience are likely to succeed, while those that rush adoption may face both regulatory and reputational risks.

Where Web3 Is Taking iGaming

Web3-enabled incentives represent a new frontier in online casino engagement. By moving beyond traditional promotions toward mission-based and co-created experiences, operators can foster deeper connections with players, improve retention, and potentially unlock new revenue streams. More broadly, the trend illustrates how leading operators are testing frameworks responsibly within regulated markets, offering a glimpse into the future of digital engagement in iGaming.

As the industry continues to evolve, the companies that successfully integrate technology, behavioral insights, and regulatory compliance into their engagement strategies are likely to stand out. For investors and industry observers alike, the shift toward Web3-enabled, incentive-driven engagement offers a compelling lens through which to evaluate the long-term growth potential of online casinos and the evolving dynamics of digital entertainment.

Benzinga Disclaimer: This article is from an unpaid external contributor. It does not represent Benzinga’s reporting and has not been edited for content or accuracy.

data.title: Ericsson Moves To Cut Up To 1,600 Jobs In Sweden
data.description:
Ericsson (NASDAQ:ERIC) stock climbed in early trading Thursday after the company revealed plans to cut jobs in Sweden.

The moves aim to sharpen the telecom gear maker's competitive edge while protecting its core tech investments.

This follows a broader workforce slide that also showed up in Ericsson's latest quarterly employee tally.

Job Reductions Planned

Ericsson said it has filed a formal notice with Swedish authorities about potential layoffs. The company estimates that up to roughly 1,600 positions across its Swedish workforce could be affected. Management has opened dialogue with local trade unions to discuss the proposal.

Why It's Happening

The company said the reductions are meant to lower structural costs while still pushing forward on critical network technology projects.

The latest move comes after a steady employee count decline over the past year.

Ericsson reported about 89,898 workers at the end of the third quarter of 2025, down from 91,937 as of June 30, 2025. A year earlier, the head count was nearly 96,000.

Market And Sector Peers

According to Benzinga Pro, ERIC stock has gained over 9% in the past year.

Investors can gain exposure to the stock via iShares U.S. Digital Infrastructure and Real Estate ETF (NYSE:IDGT).

Investors also watched peers such as Nokia Corp. (NYSE:NOK) and Cisco Systems, Inc. (NASDAQ:CSCO) trade in tandem with Ericsson. Both companies operate in global networking and telecom equipment markets and may face similar efficiency pressures.

ERIC Price Action: Ericsson shares were up 1.28% at $9.50 during premarket trading on Thursday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Photo via Shutterstock
